component.name: SidePanel
component.description: Push-content side panel system with left/right support and mobile Sheet fallback, managed via context provider.

useCases: Detail panels that push main content aside on desktop
Filter or property panels alongside data tables
Left or right auxiliary panels in dashboard layouts

requiredProps.name: SidePanelConfig.side
requiredProps.description: Which side the panel opens on

requiredProps.name: SidePanelConfig.content
requiredProps.description: The content rendered inside the panel

commonPatterns.name: provider-container-layout
commonPatterns.description: Standard setup with provider wrapping a container that hosts the main content

commonPatterns.name: open-right-panel
commonPatterns.description: Open a detail panel on the right side via the hook

commonPatterns.name: filter-panel
commonPatterns.description: Left side panel hosting a DataTableFilterPanel

antiPatterns.scenario: Using useSidePanel outside of a SidePanelProvider
antiPatterns.reason: The hook throws an error if no provider is found in the component tree
antiPatterns.alternative: Ensure SidePanelProvider wraps the component tree that uses useSidePanel

antiPatterns.scenario: Rendering SidePanelContainer without a height-constrained parent
antiPatterns.reason: The container uses flex min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-hidden and needs a parent with defined height
antiPatterns.alternative: Place SidePanelContainer inside a flex column with a defined height (e.g., h-screen or flex-1)

antiPatterns.scenario: Opening both left and right panels simultaneously on mobile
antiPatterns.reason: Mobile renders full-width sheets; two overlapping sheets create poor UX
antiPatterns.alternative: Limit to one panel at a time on mobile, or use closeAllSidePanels before opening a new one

side.description: Left panels are 18rem wide with right border; right panels are 24rem wide with left border

platform.description: Desktop renders an inline aside that pushes content; mobile renders a full-width Sheet overlay

slots.provider: SidePanelProvider wraps the tree and manages panel state; accepts pathname for auto-close on route change
slots.container: SidePanelContainer renders left panel, main content, and right panel in a flex row
slots.panelHeader: Built-in header with title, subtitle, and close button (X on desktop, ArrowLeft on mobile)
slots.panelContent: The content ReactNode from SidePanelConfig rendered in a flex column below the header

parentConstraints: Parent must have a defined height for overflow to work

interactions.open: openSidePanel({ side, title?, subtitle?, content }) renders the panel and pushes main content
interactions.close: Close button (X/ArrowLeft) or closeSidePanel(side) removes the panel
interactions.closeAll: closeAllSidePanels() closes both panels at once
interactions.routeChange: Panels auto-close when the pathname prop on SidePanelProvider changes
interactions.mobileSheet: On mobile (max-width: 767px), panels render as full-width Sheet overlays instead of inline asides

responsive.mobile: Full-width Sheet with ArrowLeft back button in header
responsive.desktop: Inline aside that pushes main content; left panel 18rem, right panel 24rem

accessibility.role: Desktop: aside with aria-label derived from title or side; Mobile: Sheet dialog
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Close button focusable via Tab; mobile Sheet supports standard dialog keyboard patterns
accessibility.screenReader: Panel title used as aria-label on the aside element; SheetTitle provided for mobile
accessibility.focusManagement: Mobile Sheet manages focus trap; desktop aside has no trap

rules: SidePanelProvider must wrap SidePanelContainer in the component tree
Panels auto-close when the pathname prop changes (route navigation)
Desktop panels push content via flex layout — they do not overlay
Mobile panels use the Sheet component with the native close button hidden
Left panels are 18rem (w-72); right panels are 24rem (w-96)
The useSidePanel hook exposes openSidePanel, closeSidePanel, closeAllSidePanels, leftPanel, and rightPanel
SidePanelConfig type is exported for consumer use
alternatives: Sheet for overlay panels that don't push content

aiHints.context: Use for panels that push main content aside on desktop and become full-width sheets on mobile. Managed entirely via context — call openSidePanel from anywhere in the tree. Pass pathname to SidePanelProvider for auto-close on navigation. Pair with DataTableFilterPanel for filter sidebars.
